messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
How long is the recorded history of the city where a short course 100 metres butterfly world record was swam in 50.59 on March 16 , 2000

content.message:
I need to find the recorded history of the city where a 100m butterfly world record was set with a time of 50.59 on March 16, 2000.

First, let me identify the key elements:
- Event: 100m butterfly world record
- Time: 50.59 seconds
- Date: March 16, 2000
- Need to find the city where this record was set
- Then determine the recorded history of that city

To start, I need to find information about this specific world record. Let me search for records related to the 100m butterfly swimming event.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "type"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain

content.message: I need to find information about the 100m butterfly world record. Let me try searching for swimming events or world records first.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. record or record progression (wd:P2869 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "world record"), also known as "olympic record", "world record":
  - links to item on the record or record progression
2. doubles record (wd:P555 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - win/lose balance for a player in doubles tournaments
3. race time (wd:P2781 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - officially recorded time elapsed in a sports race

  - first time a subject was mentioned in writing
5. maximum temperature record (wd:P6591 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "highest temperature":
  - maximum temperature reached at this location
6. Power of 10 athlete ID (wd:P2090 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "powerof10.info athlete identifier"), also known as "powerof10 athlete ID", "powerof10 athlete identifier", "Power of 10 athlete identifier", "powerof10.info athlete ID", "powerof10.info athlete identifier", etc.:
  - identifier for an athlete in the Power of 10 database
7. record held (wd:P1000 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - notable record achieved by a person or entity, include qualifiers for dates held
8. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
9. competition won (wd:P2522 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "contest won", "event won", "winner of", "won", "won event":
  - competition or sports event won by the subject
10. media legend (wd:P2096 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "audio caption", "audio description", "audio legend", "caption", "file caption", etc.:
  - qualifier to describe the media of the statement. This avoids ... media is defined at Wikidata. For the date, use P585 instead

10. doubles record (wd:P555 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):

content.message: I found the entity for 100 meters butterfly (wd:Q9127903). Now I need to find information about world records for this event. Let me search for world records related to this specific swimming event.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. record or record progression (wd:P2869 as wdt/p, matched via "world record"), also known as "olympic record", "world record":
  - links to item on the record or record progression
2. event distance (wd:P3157 as wdt/p), also known as "distance", "race distance", "stage distance":
  - distance over which a race or other event is conducted or was achieved
3. sport (wd:P641 as wdt/p), also known as "play", "plays", "sport played", "sports":
  - sport that the subject participates or participated in or is associated with
4. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "is a"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
5. subclass of (wd:P279 as wdt/p, matched via "type of"), also known as "all members of this class also belong to", "category of", "form of", "has superclass", "is a class of", etc.:
  - this item is a subclass (subset) of that item; ALL instances ... is a subclass of mountain; Everest is an instance of mountain
6. practiced by (wd:P3095 as wdt/p, matched via "P3095"), also known as "associated with", "done by", "has practitioner", "has proponent", "implemented by", etc.:
  - type of agents that study this subject or work in this field
7. uses (wd:P2283 as wdt/p, matched via "using"), also known as "instrument played", "item used", "made use of", "makes use of", "trope used", etc.:

8. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "maincat"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:

content.message: I need to find the specific world record for the 100m butterfly with a time of 50.59 on March 16, 2000. Let me search for world records that have specific times or dates.
I need to find information about the location where this record was set. Let me search for properties related to location or place of events.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. location (wd:P276 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "place"), also known as "based in", "city", "exact location", "from", "held by", etc.:
  - location of the object, structure or event; use P131 to ... entities; use P7153 for locations associated with the object
2. ranking (wd:P1352 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "place"), also known as "peak position", "place", "placing", "position", "rank":
  - subject's ordinal position as qualitatively evaluated relative to other members of a group
3. valid in place (wd:P3005 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "place"), also known as "applies to country", "applies to location", "applies to nonjurisdictional location", "applies to nonjurisdictional place", "applies to place", etc.:
  - place where a statement is valid
4. coordinate location (wd:P625 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location"), also known as "co-ordinate location", "co-ordinates", "coordinates", "co-ords", "coords", etc.:
  - geocoordinates of the subject. For Earth, please note that only the WGS84 geodetic datum is currently supported
5. work location (wd:P937 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location"), also known as "active in", "conducts business at", "conducts business in", "did work at", "did work from", etc.:
  - location where persons or organisations were actively participating in employment, business or other work
6. applies to jurisdiction (wd:P1001 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of place"), also known as "applicable area", "applicable country", "applicable geographic place", "applicable jurisdiction", "applicable location", etc.:
  - the item (institution, law, public office, public register, ... jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)
7. occupant (wd:P466 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location of"), also known as "home team", "houses", "inhabitant", "lessee", "location of", etc.:
  - person or organization occupying property
8. directions (wd:P2795 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "locality or place"), also known as "locality or place":
  - describe how to find the subject - directions, objects along way, comments
9. activity policy in this place (wd:P5023 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "place allows"), also known as "has policy", "place allows", "place forbids", "policy applies to this":
  - policy for activities in this place, for example no photos, photos allowed, no flash, lighting fires, flying drones, etc.
10. location of creation (wd:P1071 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "place made"), also known as "assembly location", "built at", "built in", "composed at", "created at", etc.:
  - place where the item was conceived or made; where applicable, location of final assembly

content.message: I need to find the specific world record for the 100m butterfly with a time of 50.59 on March 16, 2000. Let me try to find events or competitions that match this specific detail.
I need to find information about swimming events that might include specific dates and times. Let me search for properties related to dates and times in swimming events.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. point in time (wd:P585 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date"), also known as "as of", "at time", "by date", "date", "during", etc.:
  - date something took place, existed or a statement was true; for providing time use the "refine date" property (P4241)
2. day in year for periodic occurrence (wd:P837 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date"), also known as "date", "day", "day in year", "day of periodic occurrence", "day of year", etc.:
  - when a specific holiday or periodic event occurs. It can be used as statement or qualifier
3. announcement date (wd:P6949 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date"), also known as "announced date", "announced in", "announced on", "date", "date announced", etc.:
  - time of the first public presentation of a subject by the creator, of information by the media
4. start time (wd:P580 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "from date"), also known as "air date", "began", "began airing", "began broadcasting", "began streaming", etc.:
  - time an entity begins to exist or a statement starts being valid
5. date depicted (wd:P2913 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date shown"), also known as "date shown", "depicted date", "depicted time", "depicts date", "shown as at date", etc.:
  - date represented in a work (for narrative works use P2408)
6. inception (wd:P571 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date formed"), also known as "built", "commenced on date", "commencement date", "completed", "composed", etc.:
  - time when an entity begins to exist; for date of official opening use P1619
7. date of promulgation (wd:P7589 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date signed"), also known as "adoption date", "assent", "date of adoption", "date of approval", "date of assent", etc.:
  - date on which a piece of legislation formally became law (whether by assent or some other procedure)
8. name day (wd:P1750 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - day of the year associated with a first/given name. A ... that is being used. Distinguish from "feast day" (P:P841)
9. publication date (wd:P577 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date published"), also known as "air date", "airdate", "be published during", "be published in", "broadcast date", etc.:
  - date or point in time when a work or product was first published or released
10. end time (wd:P582 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "fall date"), also known as "abolished", "cease date", "cease operation", "cease time", "closed", etc.:
  - moment when an entity ceases to exist and a statement stops being entirely valid or no longer be true

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. point in time (wd:P585 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "time"), also known as "as of", "at time", "by date", "date", "during", etc.:
  - date something took place, existed or a statement was true; for providing time use the "refine date" property (P4241)
2. time index (wd:P4895 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "time point"), also known as "relative time", "temporal coordinate", "time coordinate", "time point", "timepoint", etc.:
  - indicate when something happens relative to its beginning
3. duration (wd:P2047 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "time interval"), also known as "cooking time", "cook time", "era", "interval", "length", etc.:
  - length of time of an event or process
4. refine date (wd:P4241 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "specific time"), also known as "date refinement", "exact time", "hour", "local time", "quarter", etc.:
  - qualifier to narrow down the precision of a date not fully specified by the Wikidata time datatype precision field
5. dissolved, abolished or demolished date (wd:P576 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "time abolished"), also known as "abolished", "abolishment date", "ceased date", "ceased on", "ceased publication", etc.:
  - point in time at which the subject (organisation, building) ... equipment, "discontinued date" (P2669) for stopping a product
6. start time (wd:P580 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "starttime"), also known as "air date", "began", "began airing", "began broadcasting", "began streaming", etc.:
  - time an entity begins to exist or a statement starts being valid
7. located in time zone (wd:P421 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "time observed"), also known as "observes standard time", "observes time", "standard time observed", "time observed", "time zone", etc.:
  - time zone for this item
